science.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
science.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

science.title: New quantum sensor could count individual photons and hunt dark matter
science.summary: Researchers have built an ultra-sensitive sensor capable of detecting unimaginably small amounts of energy — below one zeptojoule. The breakthrough relies on fragile superconducting materials that react to even the slightest temperature change. This level of precision could improve quantum computers, enable photon coun

science.title: Rediscovering science: New knowledge hidden in old data
science.summary: What if the knowledge that could fuel the next scientific breakthrough has simply been forgotten in an old graph or table? Valuable scientific insights may already exist across decades of published experiments, yet remain buried in old research papers, waiting to be rediscovered.

science.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
science.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researc

science.title: Scientists found a hidden Alzheimer’s trigger and shut it down
science.summary: A newly identified enzyme called IDOL could become a major new target in the fight against Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that removing it from neurons sharply reduced amyloid plaques and improved key brain processes linked to resilience and communication between cells. The discovery may lead to future treatmen

tech.title: First-generation Chromecast users stressed by devices suddenly failing
tech.summary: Google tells Ars it fixed the first-gen Chromecast bug.

tech.title: Marketer that claimed it could tap devices for ad targeting will pay $880K settlement
tech.summary: Two additional marketing companies will also pay $25,000 each.

tech.title: A hacker group is poisoning open source code at an unprecedented scale
tech.summary: GitHub is just the latest victim of TeamPCP, a gang that has carried out a spree of software supply chain attacks.

tech.title: Waymo expands pause to four cities as robotaxis keep driving into floods
tech.summary: Waymo's robotaxi service is now suspended in both Atlanta and San Antonio, as the company works to stop its vehicles from driving into flooded roads.

tech.title: Waymo halts freeway rides after robotaxis struggle in construction zones
tech.summary: The service suspension comes as Waymo has also paused operations in Atlanta and San Antonio after its robotaxis drove into flooded streets.

medicine.title: A once-daily pill takes aim at measles, croup and other dangerous viruses
medicine.summary: A new oral antiviral drug candidate has been developed for the treatment of diseases caused by orthoparamyxoviruses, such as measles and croup syndrome, according to a study published by researchers in the Center for Translational Antiviral Research at Georgia State University. The paper in Science Advances identifies

medicine.title: Scientists reverse Alzheimer’s in mice with breakthrough nanotechnology
medicine.summary: A new nanotechnology treatment reversed Alzheimer’s symptoms in mice by restoring the brain’s natural cleanup system. The specially engineered nanoparticles helped clear toxic amyloid proteins from the brain and repair the blood-brain barrier, which normally protects and regulates the brain’s environment. In one striki

medicine.title: New indicator for response to therapy in pediatric cancers identified
medicine.summary: A study by researchers at the University of Birmingham has identified a new biomarker for response to a specific cancer therapy, treating children with Ewing Sarcoma and other tumor types. The study, which is a Phase I/II treatment arm within the eSMART Trial, was carried out at the Cancer Research UK Clinical Trials U

medicine.title: Weight loss drug semaglutide helps patients who do not respond to bariatric surgery, research finds
medicine.summary: Metabolic/bariatric surgery is a highly effective treatment for people living with severe obesity and/or metabolic health conditions, which works through changing the anatomy of the digestive system and thereby changing the way it functions and the hormones it produces. Procedures include the sleeve gastrectomy and gas

medicine.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics
mathematics.summary: Grothendieck is revered in the world of math; outside of it, he’s known for his unusual life, if he’s known at all. But what were his actual mathematical contributions? mathematics.title: What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean?
mathematics.summary: At 25, Kurt Gödel proved there can never be a mathematical “theory of everything.” Columnist Natalie Wolchover explores the implications. The post What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Unknowable Math Can Help Hide Secrets
mathematics.summary: A graduate student recently harnessed the complexity of mathematical proofs to create a powerful new tool in cryptography. The post How Unknowable Math Can Help Hide Secrets first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: This cryptocurrency billionaire will fly SpaceX's 1st private Starship to Mars, but when?
space.summary: SpaceX says it will launch a private flyby mission to Mars that will be led by cryptocurrency billionaire Chun Wang, but when it will fly is unclear.

space.title: Starship V3 from space: Satellite snaps amazing photo of SpaceX megarocket on the pad
space.summary: A sharp-eyed satellite captured an amazing photo of SpaceX's first Starship V3 megarocket on the pad Thursday (May 21), just before it was supposed to lift off.

space.title: SpaceX's 1st Starship V3 megarocket launch scrubbed at last minute. Sorry, Nicki Minaj
space.summary: SpaceX's new "V3" Starship megarocket was supposed to fly for the first time ever on Thursday (May 21), but technical issues nixed that plan.

space.title: Rocket Lab launches private Japanese Earth-observing satellite to orbit (video)
space.summary: Rocket Lab launched an Earth-observing radar satellite for the Japanese company Synspective early Friday morning (May 22).

space.title: 'Stop lying!' Conspiracy theorist confronts Artemis 2 astronauts, accuses them of faking their moon mission
space.summary: A conspiracy theorist confronted the Artemis 2 astronauts on Capitol Hill recently, accusing them of faking their moon mission. They showed more restraint than Buzz Aldrin did in a similar situation.

astronomy.title: How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form?
astronomy.summary: A planetary odd couple — a mini-Neptune and a hot Jupiter — probably formed much farther away from their star before migrating closer in. The post How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31
astronomy.summary: Venus and Jupiter — the Evening Star and the False Evening Star — draw closer together in the western twilight and point down to Mercury. The waxing Moon shows off its rich telescopic detail. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Moon and Planets to Gather in Twilight Spectacle on May 18–20
astronomy.summary: Watch the crescent Moon dance with the planets when it returns next week. The post Moon and Planets to Gather in Twilight Spectacle on May 18–20 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: A Beautiful Death: How a Dying Star Created the Crystal Ball Nebula
astronomy.summary: Planetary nebula are created when a dying star sheds it outer layers. The gas is lit up by the star and all the gorgeous, changing detail is exposed. astronomy.title: Supermassive Black Holes Can Render Exoplanets Uninhabitable at Great Distances
astronomy.summary: Life on Earth relies on energy from astrophysical sources. But what if the astrophysical source isn't a star, but a supermassive black hole and its active galactic nuclei? architecture.title: How the Christo and Jeanne-Claude Center is Breathing New Life into Gabrovo’s Cultural Identity
architecture.summary: Large factories are being transformed into museums, former administrative buildings are becoming co-working spaces, and even churches are being converted into homes. architecture.title: O4 House / studiokian
architecture.summary: In dense suburban environments, the need for privacy often comes into conflict with the desire for openness and natural light. architecture.title: Westlake Dermatology Georgetown / Matt Fajkus Architecture
architecture.summary: This medical office building was designed to be an embodiment of the Westlake Dermatology brand, "the gold standard for cosmetic dermatology". With its prominent location on Williams Drive, the building's crisp modern form and clean, well detailed materials serves as a billboard for the WD brand aesthetic and quality.

architecture.title: Translucent Centre Pompidou Hanwha set to open in Seoul
architecture.summary: The Centre Pompidou Hanwha designed by French architecture studio Wilmotte & Associés has been revealed ahead of its opening in Seoul, South Korea, next month. Set to open in Seoul's financial district on 4 June, the four-storey building contains two large galleries alongside a cafe, shop, restaurant and auditorium

architecture.title: Kengo Kuma & Associates designs its first museum building in the US for Pennsylvania
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Kengo Kuma & Associates and landscape design office Field Operations have released designs for a vernacular expansion to the Brandywine Conservancy & Museum of Art outside of Philadelphia, USA. construction.title: The top commercial contractors of 2026: ENR
construction.summary: Turner Construction remained No. 1 for another year, while Mortenson leapt 12 spots to crack the top 10 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 list.

construction.title: Maryland unveils 4 Key Bridge contracts totaling over $4B
construction.summary: On Tuesday, the Maryland Transportation Authority outlined the four separate procurements for various aspects of the Baltimore bridge’s reconstruction.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Construction starts jumped in April as more project types broke ground
construction.summary: Unlike recent months, groundbreakings stretched beyond a handful of data center and energy megaprojects, according to Dodge Construction Network.

ai.title: What Parameter Golf taught us about AI-assisted research
ai.summary: Parameter Golf brought together 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions to explore AI-assisted machine learning research, coding agents, quantization, and novel model design under strict constraints.
